title: ABI Summer School 2026
text: The aim is to provide comprehensive training on bioinformatics computational resources, and domain-specific tools and workflows to improve participants’ proficiency in executing, interpreting, and communicating analyses for major bioinformatics studies, with a special focus on genomics.

title: Contributing
text: We welcome contributions of all sizes — bug reports, documentation improvements, or new features.

title: Contributing
text:
Getting started

Fork the repository on GitHub and clone your fork locally.
Install dependencies with npm install.
Create a feature branch: git checkout -b feat/my-feature.

title: Contributing
text:
Development workflow
npm run dev      # start the dev server with hot reload
npm test         # run the full test suite
npm run lint     # check code style
npm run build    # build for production






Important



Please ensure all tests pass (npm test) before opening a pull request.

title: Contributing
text:
Pull request guidelines

Keep PRs focused — one feature or fix per PR.
Write or update tests for any changed behaviour.
Update the relevant documentation in the docs/ folder.
Use a clear, descriptive PR title (e.g. feat: add lazy() timeout option).

title: Contributing
text:
Commit style
We follow Conventional Commits:



Prefix
Use for




feat:
New features


fix:
Bug fixes


docs:
Documentation only


chore:
Tooling, CI, dependencies


refactor:
Code changes with no behaviour change

title: Contributing
text:
Code of conduct
This project follows the Contributor Covenant. Be kind and constructive.

title: Configuration
text:
Create a myproject.config.js in your project root:
export default {
  theme: 'warm',        // 'warm' | 'cool' | 'neutral'
  plugins: [],          // array of plugin instances
  debug: false,         // enable verbose logging
  output: './dist',     // build output directory
}






Tip



All config keys are optional — MyProject ships with sensible defaults for every option.

title: Configuration
text:
Create a myproject.config.js in your project root:
export default {
  theme: 'warm',        // 'warm' | 'cool' | 'neutral'
  plugins: [],          // array of plugin instances
  debug: false,         // enable verbose logging
  output: './dist',     // build output directory
}






Tip



All config keys are optional — MyProject ships with sensible defaults for every option.

title: Configuration
text:
Options reference



Key
Type
Default
Description




theme
string
'neutral'
Visual theme preset


plugins
array
[]
Plugin instances to load


debug
boolean
false
Enable verbose console output


output
string
'./dist'
Build output directory

title: Configuration
text:
Environment variables
You can also configure MyProject via environment variables, which take precedence over the config file:
MYPROJECT_THEME=cool
MYPROJECT_DEBUG=true

title: Basic Usage
Basic Usage
Basic Usage
text:
Working with data
Use the store module for reactive state:
import { store } from 'myproject'

const state = store({ count: 0 })

state.subscribe(s => console.log('count:', s.count))
state.set({ count: state.get().count + 1 })

title: Basic Usage
text:
Lifecycle events
MyProject emits the following events you can hook into with app.on():



Event
When it fires




init
After the instance is created


ready
After app.run() completes


error
When an unhandled error occurs


destroy
Before the instance is torn down









Tip



You can also use app.once() for one-time event handlers that auto-remove themselves after firing.

title: FAQ
text: Yes. MyProject is framework-agnostic. Official adapters exist for React, Vue 3, and Svelte — see the Projects page for links.

title: FAQ
text: Yes. MyProject is framework-agnostic. Official adapters exist for React, Vue 3, and Svelte — see the Projects page for links.

title: FAQ
text:
Is it production ready?
Absolutely. MyProject is used in production by dozens of teams. The core API has been stable since v2.0, and breaking changes follow a deprecation period of at least one major version.

title: FAQ
section: Does it work server-side (Node.js / Deno / Bun)?
text:
Does it work server-side (Node.js / Deno / Bun)?
Yes, MyProject is isomorphic. The core library and store module work in any JS runtime. Some browser-specific plugins (e.g. DOM adapters) will only work in browser environments.

title: FAQ
section: How do I report a bug?
text:
How do I report a bug?
Open an issue on GitHub. Please include your Node version, MyProject version, and a minimal reproduction.

title: FAQ
text:
Is there a changelog?
Yes — see CHANGELOG.md on GitHub for a full history of releases.

title: FAQ
section: Can I use it without a bundler?
text:
Can I use it without a bundler?
Yes, via the CDN build — see Installation. For production we recommend using a bundler to benefit from tree-shaking.

title: API Reference
text:
Creates and returns a new app instance.
function create(config?: AppConfig): App
Parameters

config (optional) — A partial AppConfig object. Defaults are applied for any missing keys.

Returns — An App instance.

title: API Reference
API Reference
text:
app.on(event, handler)
Registers a lifecycle event handler.
app.on(event: string, handler: Function): void

title: API Reference
text:
app.off(event, handler)
Removes a previously registered handler.
app.off(event: string, handler: Function): void

title: API Reference
text:
app.once(event, handler)
Registers a one-time handler that removes itself after firing.
app.once(event: string, handler: Function): void

title: API Reference
API Reference
text:
store(initial)
Creates a reactive store.
function store<T>(initial: T): Store<T>

Store methods







Method
Description




store.get()
Returns the current state


store.set(partial)
Merges partial into state and notifies subscribers


store.subscribe(fn)
Registers a subscriber; returns an unsubscribe function


store.reset()
Resets state to the initial value

Installation
text: MyProject requires Node.js ≥ 18 and a modern package manager. It runs in both browser and server environments.

title: Installation
text: MyProject requires Node.js ≥ 18 and a modern package manager. It runs in both browser and server environments.

title: Installation
text:
npm / yarn / pnpm
npm install myproject        # npm
yarn add myproject           # yarn
pnpm add myproject           # pnpm

title: Installation
text:
CDN (no bundler)
For quick prototyping without a build step:
<script type="module">
  import { create } from 'https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/myproject/+esm'
</script>






Note



CDN builds are not recommended for production. Use a bundler for tree-shaking and optimal bundle size.

title: Installation
text:
Verify the installation
import { create } from 'myproject'
console.log(create.version) // e.g. "2.4.1"
If you see a version number, you’re good to go. Head to Configuration next.

title: Projects
text:
The heart of the ecosystem — a small, stable, well-tested core that everything else builds on. The core library is intentionally minimal so it can be used in any environment without assumptions about your framework or build tool.
View on GitHub

title: Projects
text:
Official Plugins

myproject-react
React hooks and context for seamless integration with React 18+.
npm install @myproject/react
useState-style hooks, <Provider> component, and full TypeScript support.
GitHub · react hooks typescript



myproject-vue
Composables and plugin for Vue 3 with full reactivity support.
npm install @myproject/vue
Uses Vue’s ref and computed under the hood for zero-overhead reactivity.
GitHub · vue3 composables



myproject-svelte
Svelte stores and actions for effortless state synchronisation.
npm install @myproject/svelte
Wraps store as a native Svelte writable so you can use the $ prefix syntax.
GitHub · svelte stores
